stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers and text-generation.
The mention of Llama indicates relevance to the competitive landscape of large language models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core field of large language models (LLMs) and text generation.
The model utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The entry highlights the DeepSeek-R1 model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
The associated tags indicate relevance to transformers and deepseek_v3 architecture.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI tags including 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware.
This represents a significant step in optimizing local AI inference on Apple platforms.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables the use of various models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) locally or via the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are permissively licensed under Apache 2.0.
This partnership with OpenAI and ROOST aims to bring advanced safety tools to the open-source community.

stories.title: Course correction: Google to link more sources in AI Overviews
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is enhancing its AI Overviews feature by improving source citation.
This represents a 'course correction' in Google's AI search strategy.
The changes aim to provide users with more transparent and traceable information from linked sources.

stories.title: ActCam: Zero-Shot Joint Camera and 3D Motion Control for Video Generation
stories.author: Baptiste Bellot-Gurlet
bullet_points: ActCam is a zero-shot method designed for advanced video generation, addressing the need for fine-grained control over both character motion and camera cinematography.
It jointly transfers character motion from a source video into a novel scene while allowing per-frame control over intrinsic and extrinsic camera parameters.
The model is designed to be highly adaptable, building upon any pre-trained image-to-video diffusion model conditioned on scene depth.

stories.title: Why Global LLM Leaderboards Are Misleading: Small Portfolios for Heterogeneous Supervised ML
stories.author: Swati Gupta
bullet_points: The paper critiques current LLM leaderboards, which rely on pairwise human feedback (like Arena).
Analysis of 89K comparisons across 52 LLMs shows that the standard global Bradley-Terry (BT) ranking is misleading.
The study found that the top 50 models ranked by BT are statistically indistinguishable, with win probabilities barely exceeding 0.53.

stories.title: Cited but Not Verified: Parsing and Evaluating Source Attribution in LLM Deep Research Agents
stories.author: Corey Feld
bullet_points: LLM-powered research agents struggle with verifiable source attribution, despite synthesizing information from numerous web sources.
Current methods either rely on potentially biased self-citation or use RAG without validating source accessibility or factual consistency.
The paper introduces a novel evaluation framework utilizing an AST parser to address the critical issue of source attribution reliability in LLMs.

stories.title: Obsidian plugin was abused to deploy a remote access trojan
stories.author: cmbailey
bullet_points: A security vulnerability in an Obsidian plugin was exploited.
The abuse allowed for the deployment of a Remote Access Trojan (RAT).
This highlights the security risks associated with third-party plugins in personal knowledge management tools.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to run tools like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source models (via Ollama) and proprietary models (like Claude) via API compatibility.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code locally.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: The new Wild West of AI kids’ toys
stories.author: Sophie Charara, WIRED.com
bullet_points: New AI-powered children's toys are emerging as a significant market trend.
These connected companions have the potential to disrupt traditional forms of play and storytelling.
The rapid development of these toys has prompted calls from lawmakers for potential bans.

stories.title: Manufacturing qubits that can move
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: The article addresses the technical challenge of integrating electronic manufacturing with flexible geometries.
The focus is on the difficulty of manufacturing mobile or movable qubits.
This points to ongoing hurdles in scaling and engineering quantum computing hardware.

stories.title: UniPool: A Globally Shared Expert Pool for Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Hong Cheng
bullet_points: Introduces UniPool, a novel approach to Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures.
Challenges the conventional MoE design where each transformer layer has isolated expert capacity.
Suggests that replacing layer-specific routers with uniform random routing causes minimal accuracy degradation, implying a potential shift in expert allocation strategies.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: LLMs are good at solving problems but struggle to generate novel, valid, and challenging problems for training.
The paper introduces VHG, a method that uses verifiers to generate hard mathematical problems, addressing the limitations of human-only or naive self-play methods.
This research aims to improve LLM training and facilitate autonomous scientific research by providing high-quality problem sets.

stories.title: Optimizer-Model Consistency: Full Finetuning with the Same Optimizer as Pretraining Forgets Less
stories.author: Tong Zhang
bullet_points: The paper investigates the role of optimizers in LLM training, specifically comparing pretraining and finetuning.
It proposes that using the exact same optimizer during full finetuning as was used during pretraining leads to a better learning-forgetting tradeoff.
This approach reportedly outperforms other optimizers and even LoRA during Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT).

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established benchmark or ground-truth labels exist for a specific domain.
Introduces the concept of 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' to formalize this evaluation setting.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can serve as deployment evidence.

stories.title: Superintelligent Retrieval Agent: The Next Frontier of Information Retrieval
stories.author: Anshumali Shrivastava
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of current retrieval-augmented agents treating information retrieval as a 'black box'.
Existing agents use iterative, exploratory querying, mimicking a novice user rather than an expert.
The proposed work aims to improve efficiency by incorporating strong priors, reducing retrieval rounds and latency.

stories.title: Are We Making Progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization? A Comprehensive Benchmark Study
stories.author: Olga Fink
bullet_points: The paper addresses the lack of clarity regarding genuine progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization (MMDG).
Current research suffers from fragmentation due to inconsistent evaluation protocols across studies.
Existing benchmarks are criticized for focusing too heavily on action recognition while neglecting broader real-world applications.

stories.title: EMO: Pretraining Mixture of Experts for Emergent Modularity
stories.author: Sewon Min
bullet_points: The paper addresses the inefficiency of monolithic Large Language Models (LLMs) that require the full model for narrow tasks.
It investigates Mixture-of-Experts (MoEs) as a solution to activate only relevant model components.
The research highlights a practical limitation of current MoE implementations: restricting inference to a subset of experts causes significant performance degradation.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation in LLMs: difficulty in generating valid, challenging, and novel mathematical problems.
Proposes VHG, a new method that incorporates verification to prevent the generation of invalid problems.
Aims to advance LLM training and enable autonomous scientific research by improving problem creation.

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established benchmark or ground-truth labels exist for a specific context.
Introduces the concept of 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' to formalize this evaluation setting.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can be considered valid deployment evidence.

stories.title: StraTA: Incentivizing Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Strategic Trajectory Abstraction
stories.author: Zhenfei Yin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of optimizing LLMs for long-horizon decision-making in agentic RL.
Introduces StraTA, a framework that incorporates explicit trajectory-level strategy into RL.
Aims to improve exploration and credit assignment over extended decision trajectories.

stories.title: Recursive Agent Optimization
stories.author: Graham Neubig
bullet_points: Introduces Recursive Agent Optimization (RAO), a new RL approach for training self-replicating agents.
These recursive agents can spawn and delegate sub-tasks to new instances of themselves.
RAO enables agents to scale to longer contexts and tackle complex problems using a divide-and-conquer strategy.

stories.title: Can RL Teach Long-Horizon Reasoning to LLMs? Expressiveness Is Key
stories.author: Abulhair Saparov
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation in LLM reasoning research: the lack of controlled, scalable training environments.
Introduces 'ScaleLogic,' a synthetic framework designed to independently control two critical aspects of reasoning difficulty: proof planning depth (horizon) and logical expressiveness.
Focuses on using Reinforcement Learning (RL) to systematically study how LLMs scale their reasoning capabilities for long-horizon tasks.

stories.title: MASPO: Joint Prompt Optimization for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Min Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MASPO, a new framework for optimizing prompts in LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems (MAS).
Addresses the challenge of aligning local agent objectives with overall system goals in complex collaborative tasks.
Focuses on the difficulty of jointly optimizing role-specific prompts across interacting agents.

stories.title: Debian must ship reproducible packages
stories.author: lists.debian.org via fanf
bullet_points: Debian is facing a requirement to ship reproducible packages.
This relates to the technical standard of ensuring software builds are consistent across different environments.
The discussion is within the Linux/open-source community, indicating moderate but important technical relevance.

stories.title: Introducing a OWASP Game for threat modeling Agentic AI, Cloud, Devops, Frontend, LLM, Automation, and Web
stories.author: sydseter
bullet_points: Introduces an OWASP Game designed for threat modeling.
The game covers a broad range of modern tech domains including Agentic AI, Cloud, DevOps, and LLMs.
It promotes a 'shift-left' security approach starting before code scanning.

stories.title: I Built Tautest: A Mutation Testing Workflow for AI-Written Tests
stories.author: canblmz
bullet_points: Tautest is an open-source tool designed to improve the quality of tests generated by AI.
It leverages StrykerJS to perform mutation testing, specifically targeting weak or insufficient AI-written tests.
The tool integrates with GitHub Actions and generates AI-ready prompts to assist developers in fixing identified test weaknesses.
